title: Short Break From The Heat Before Extreme Heat Returns - KOTA Territory News
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
full_text:
RAPID CITY, S.D. (KOTA) -
Another mild night is in store for KOTA Territory as partly clear skies take over. Low temperatures manage to drop into the mid to upper 60s around KOTA Territory tonight. Tomorrow, partly sunny skies are likely as we see temperatures stay closer to average. Forecasted highs in the mid to upper 80s are in place for Tuesday. There could be a few showers and storms late Tuesday night into early Wednesday morning.
Wednesday, scattered showers and storms are possible early in the morning, but another chance for some isolated storms are possible in the afternoon and evening. High temperatures stay near average with mid 80s in the forecast. Thursday, widespread showers and thunderstorms are possible around the region as we see high temperatures slightly below average for the first time in what feels like forever. All good things must come to an end as we start to heat right back up by the end of the work week.
Friday, we are first alerting you to the potential for extreme heat to return to KOTA Territory. Highs will be just under 100 degrees in many locations. Rapid City has a forecasted high of 97. Saturday is looking to be the hottest where temperatures will get into the lower triple digits, with a forecasted high of 104 for Rapid City. A first alert weather day will likely have to be called this weekend. Sunday, we see another day over the century mark with highs in the lower 100s. More heat is expected to start out next week as we see a high of 100 forecasted for Rapid City.
